runs.content:
Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($477.08) — up ~24% over the trailing month on the $10B Crinetics Pharmaceuticals acquisition and a UBS price-target hike to $585, a momentum entry rather than another earnings-day gap-chase (Strategy D was used yesterday on ASML). No exits today; the broad chip-sector selloff eased MU (+9.74%→+0.94%) and AMAT (+30.02%→+26.47%), both with T1/T2 already banked.

First Horizon (FHN) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (−0.9% gap) after a clean Q2 earnings beat (EPS $0.54 vs $0.53 est., net income +12% YoY) drove ~3.0× average volume on Jul 15 — a cleaner signal than BlackRock's own Q2 beat, which only ran ~1.9× average volume, just under the 2× threshold.

ASML picked up in Portfolio 1 at the previous close ($1,843.04) — set the day before today's Q2 earnings beat (€9.33B revenue) and second FY26 guidance raise of the year (to €43–45B on AI demand), so entry captures the pre-announcement price. CSCO hit its 60-day max hold and closed flat (−0.95%); CRWD triggered T1 (+27.05%); IBM repriced sharply to −3.52% after yesterday's Q2 profit warning.

CleanSpark (CLSK) entered Portfolio 2 at today's open (+2.9% gap) after a 20-year, $6.6B data-centre lease (Sandersville, GA, extendable to $11.6B) drove an elevated-volume +8.8% session on Jul 14 — the cleanest single-stock catalyst available versus the more diffuse semiconductor-sector rebound or the multi-day oil-refiner rally.
